paper_title: Study Site, Samples and Environmental Parameters

methods.description: Monitoring of environmental conditions in Pau d' Alho and Curupira caves to assess temperature, humidity, and CO2 levels, which are crucial for understanding the conditions under which stalagmites form.

steps.description: The monitoring performed during a 13 month period in Pau d' Alho cave shows that the air in the hall where the sample ALHO6 was collected was constantly saturated, while the relative humidity at the cave entrance was 33% during the dry months (August-September) and around 90% during the wet season.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor relative humidity in Pau d' Alho cave over 13 months

steps.description: The temperature was monitored over a period of two years and in the hall showed a minimum of 25.5 ± 0.1 °C and a maximum of 26.0 ± 0.1 °C, while at the entrance of this cave the temperature varied between 16.0 ± 0.1 °C and 26.8 ± 0.1 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor temperature in Pau d' Alho cave over two years

steps.description: In Curupira cave, the temperature was monitored during 10 months and the values in the hall where the stalagmite CUR4 was collected were consistently at 25.7 ± 0.1 °C during all of the monitored period, with the relative humidity at a constant 100% during the entire monitoring period.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor temperature and humidity in Curupira cave over 10 months

steps.description: Both caves are characterized by high CO2 concentrations in the halls where the stalagmites were collected (> 1000 ppm in Pau d' Alho cave and > 500 ppm in Curupira cave), as evidenced by repeated measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: measure CO2 concentrations in Pau d' Alho and Curupira caves

methods.method_name: Isotopic Analysis of Rainfall and Drip Water
methods.description: Analysis of isotopic composition of rainfall and cave drip water to understand the relationship between precipitation and isotopic records in speleothems.

steps.description: Observational data from the Global Network of Isotopes in Precipitation program of the International Atomic Energy Agency (GNIP-IAEA) confirm the negative correlation on interannual time scales between monthly anomalies of precipitation and monthly δ18O anomalies at a station in Cuiabá city.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch isotopic data from GNIP-IAEA for Cuiabá city

steps.description: The δ18O and δD values of rainwater from the GNIP program in Cuiabá, as well as the δ18O and δD values of rainwater and cave drip water collected by the authors during both dry and wet seasons in Pau d' Alho and Curupira caves, all plot on top of the global meteoric water line (GMWL).
steps.searchable_summary: collect and analyze δ18O and δD values of rainwater and drip water
